title: Power bank and flashlight discipline
summary: A simple priority system for lighting and charging during long outages.
content: Battery power disappears when every device is treated as equal. Decide which phone stays most charged, which light is used for common areas, and when devices are checked. Keep brightness low, disable unneeded radios and background tasks, and write down charging order. Store batteries where children cannot short them with metal objects.
materials: Notebook. Context: “Power bank and flashlight discipline”.
Pencil. Context: “Power bank and flashlight discipline”.
Labels. Context: “Power bank and flashlight discipline”.
Clean storage space. Context: “Power bank and flashlight discipline”.
steps: Keep entertainment use separate from emergency power.
warnings: Do not leave damaged batteries charging unattended.
Do not use open flame just because batteries were wasted.
common_mistakes: Treating Power bank and flashlight discipline as finished after the first attempt instead of writing the next review time.
Moving people or supplies for Power bank and flashlight discipline without checking vulnerable people, exits, and safe limits first.
Mixing clean and unsafe items during Power bank and flashlight discipline because labels, dates, or responsible names were not written down.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Power bank and flashlight discipline with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Power bank and flashlight discipline if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Power bank and flashlight discipline and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Power bank and flashlight discipline or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Power bank and flashlight discipline are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Power bank and flashlight discipline”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Power bank and flashlight discipline as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Power bank and flashlight discipline when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Power bank and flashlight discipline.
short_term: For the first hours of Power bank and flashlight discipline, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Power bank and flashlight discipline remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Power bank and flashlight discipline fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Power bank and flashlight discipline to the real environment: check water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
